examples.title: Pipe Communication (Parent-Child)
examples.description: Demonstrates unnamed pipe communication between parent and child processes
examples.explanation: Creates an unnamed pipe before fork. Parent writes to write end, child reads from read end. File descriptors must be properly closed in each process.

examples.title: Producer-Consumer with Semaphores
examples.description: Implements producer-consumer problem using Python threading and semaphores
examples.code:
import threading
import time
import random

# Shared buffer
buffer = []
BUFFER_SIZE = 5

# Semaphores
empty = threading.Semaphore(BUFFER_SIZE)  # Count empty slots
full = threading.Semaphore(0)              # Count filled slots
mutex = threading.Lock()                    # Mutual exclusion

def producer(item):
    empty.acquire()
    mutex.acquire()
    
    # Add item to buffer
    buffer.append(item)
    print(f"Produced: {item}")
    
    mutex.release()
    full.release()

def consumer():
    full.acquire()
    mutex.acquire()
    
    # Remove item from buffer
    item = buffer.pop(0)
    print(f"Consumed: {item}")
    
    mutex.release()
    empty.release()

# Test with producers and consumers
producers = [threading.Thread(target=producer, args=(i,)) for i in range(5)]
consumers = [threading.Thread(target=consumer) for _ in range(5)]

for p in producers:
    p.start()
for c in consumers:
    c.start()
for t in producers + consumers:
    t.join()

print("All operations completed")
examples.explanation: Uses three semaphores: empty (counts available slots), full (counts filled slots), and mutex (ensures mutual exclusion). Producer decrements empty, increments full. Consumer does reverse.

examples.title: Shared Memory Communication
examples.description: Demonstrates shared memory IPC between two processes
examples.explanation: Writer creates shared memory segment using shmget and ftok. Both processes attach to same segment using shmat. Writer can write, reader reads (read-only). Must synchronize manually!

examples.title: Message Queue using PipedInputStream/PipedOutputStream
examples.description: Demonstrates message-based communication between threads in Java
examples.explanation: Uses PipedInputStream and PipedOutputStream connected together. Producer writes messages, consumer reads them. Similar to Unix pipes but works between threads in Java.
